content.title: Your Injector's Worst Nightmare Just Got Published
content.preview: A new JAMA Derm paper quantifies exactly how often filler goes wrong. Plus: the collagen peptide trial that broke TikTok, and Westman Atelier's new lip product is quietly brilliant.
content.but_first: JAMA Dermatology just published the largest-ever retrospective on filler complications and the numbers are worse than your injector told you.

quote_of_day.text: Beauty is the promise of happiness.
quote_of_day.attribution: — Stendhal, which feels more like a threat when you're reading complication rates

injection_report.headline: The Largest Filler Complication Study Ever Just Dropped
injection_report.body: **What's going on:** A JAMA Dermatology retrospective published March 30 analyzed over 14,000 dermal filler procedures across 23 clinics and found a 2.1% vascular occlusion rate, nearly triple the 0.7% figure the industry has been citing from smaller datasets. Calcium hydroxylapatite fillers showed the highest incidence. **What it means:** The old safety stats were drawn from manufacturer-sponsored trials with cherry-picked patient pools. This is real-world, messy-patient, high-volume data, and it suggests the conversation around informed consent has been inadequate for years. Board-certified derm or plastic surgeon offices had roughly half the complication rate of med spas. **What's next:** Ask your injector if they stock hyaluronidase on-site (and if it's CaHA they're using, ask what their occlusion protocol is, because hyaluronidase won't dissolve it).
injection_report.highlight: The single best screening question before any filler appointment: 'What is your vascular occlusion protocol and do you keep emergency supplies in-office?' If they hesitate, leave.
injection_report.related: The full JAMA Dermatology retrospective on filler adverse events across 23 clinics and what the 2.1% vascular occlusion rate actually means for patients(URL_PLACEHOLDER)

put_it_in_your_mouth.headline: The Collagen Peptide Trial That Has Derms Rewriting Their Scripts
put_it_in_your_mouth.body: **What's going on:** A double-blind RCT published March 31 in Nutrients followed 120 women ages 35 to 55 taking 10g of specific collagen dipeptides (prolyl-hydroxyproline and hydroxyprolyl-glycine) daily for 12 weeks. The treatment group showed a 22% increase in skin elasticity and a measurable reduction in perioral fine lines versus placebo. **What that means:** This isn't the vague "collagen powder" haze. The study isolated two dipeptides and used validated cutometry, which means your smoothie collagen might do nothing if it doesn't contain these specific fragments. Look for supplements listing prolyl-hydroxyproline on the label. **This week's cheat meal:** A charred miso-glazed salmon rice bowl from your nearest Japanese spot. The glycine and proline in the skin are basically a collagen precursor delivery system you can eat with chopsticks.
put_it_in_your_mouth.highlight: Most collagen supplements don't disclose their dipeptide profile. Look specifically for prolyl-hydroxyproline (PO) and hydroxyprolyl-glycine (OG) on the label. If it just says 'hydrolyzed collagen,' it's a gamble.
put_it_in_your_mouth.cheat_meal: Charred miso-glazed salmon rice bowl. Order it with the skin on. Crispy salmon skin is basically edible collagen scaffolding dressed in umami.
put_it_in_your_mouth.related: The full Nutrients RCT on collagen dipeptides and perioral skin elasticity, including which peptide fragments actually survived digestion(URL_PLACEHOLDER)

lip_lab.headline: Westman Atelier's New Lip Suede Is Doing Something Different With Squalane
lip_lab.body: **What's going on:** Westman Atelier quietly launched Lip Suede on March 30, a matte lip color built on a squalane-and-jojoba base rather than the typical wax matrix. **Why it matters:** Most matte lipsticks dehydrate because they rely on volatile silicones to set. This formula skips them entirely, using plant-derived squalane as the carrier, which means the matte finish develops as the oils absorb rather than as solvents evaporate. It's a genuinely different engineering approach. **The verdict:** Soft, buildable, surprisingly hydrating for a true matte. Best suited for anyone who quit mattes because of the flaking. Eight shades at launch. Not cheap at $58, but the formula earns it.
lip_lab.highlight: If you gave up on matte lip color because every formula cracked by noon, this is the re-entry point. The squalane carrier changes the wear pattern entirely.
lip_lab.related: How squalane-based lip formulas differ from volatile silicone mattes at the molecular level and why your lips care(URL_PLACEHOLDER)

lips_in_6.text: The American Med Spa Association released updated March 2026 safety guidelines recommending ultrasound verification before all mid-face filler injections. Adoption is voluntary, which tells you everything.

A March 29 preprint in Cell Metabolism found that time-restricted eating (16:8) reduced inflammatory markers linked to periodontal disease by 19% in a 200-person cohort. Your gums have a clock, apparently.
Tower 28 just expanded its SOS Lip Treatment into three tinted shades, launching April 1. Same hypochlorous acid base, now with sheer color.

A Nature Communications paper published March 31 identified a new mechanism by which UV exposure degrades lip vermillion border collagen at twice the rate of surrounding facial skin. SPF lip balm discourse: vindicated again.
The EU SCCS flagged titanium dioxide nanoparticles in lip products as warranting re-evaluation in a March 30 opinion update. No ban yet, but the dossier is open.

Zendaya and Tom Holland were photographed at a Louvre private viewing on March 29 and the internet's sole focus was her lip liner. It's reportedly Charlotte Tilbury Pillow Talk Intense, which tells you nothing about the Louvre.

rotation_1.text: We've been using the Dermalogica Daily Microfoliant on lips once a week before applying treatment balm. Sounds aggressive, looks transformative. The rice-based enzyme is gentle enough for vermillion tissue and it clears every flake without irritation. Just don't overdo it.

The Laneige Lip Glowy Balm in Berry is the only tinted balm we've repurchased three times this year. Sheer enough for video calls, moisturizing enough to skip lip treatment underneath. $19 and earns every dollar. *Keep scrolling…more recs below*

quick_hits.label: Best Kiss
quick_hits.text: Beyoncé's Cowboy Carter anniversary tour kicked off March 29 in Houston and her custom Pat McGrath lip in a deep burgundy-black was mixed live backstage. The shade doesn't exist for purchase, which is the most Beyoncé thing imaginable.

quick_hits.label: Keep It Out
quick_hits.text: Stop using high-pH baking soda toothpaste if you get cold sores. A small NYU pilot study published this week found that alkaline oral pH above 8.5 correlated with more frequent HSV-1 reactivation in the 40-person cohort. Preliminary, but easy enough to avoid.

The ADA released a March 30 statement endorsing hydroxyapatite toothpaste as a fluoride alternative for adults with enamel erosion. This is a significant shift from their previous fluoride-only position and validates what Japanese dentistry has practiced for decades.

Seed announced a reformulation of DS-01 on April 1 that adds a Limosilactobacillus reuteri strain linked to oral microbiome balance in two recent RCTs. Your probiotic might actually do something for your mouth now.

on_our_calendar.text: April 2: AAD releases its updated 2026 consensus guidelines on combination neuromodulator and filler protocols. Watch for new spacing recommendations.
April 3: Sephora's Spring Savings Event early access opens for Rouge members. Lip category is 20% off, which makes that Westman Atelier Lip Suede $46.40.
April 4: World Vitamin C Day, which is made up, but The Ordinary is doing a 25% site-wide sale and their Lip Gloss is quietly excellent at $8.

rotation_2.text: The Supergoop Lipscreen SPF 40 is the only sunscreen lip product that doesn't taste like a chemistry set. We keep one in every bag. Non-negotiable for anyone who read that Nature Communications UV study above.

Rhode's Peptide Lip Tint in Toast keeps selling out for a reason. The peptide complex actually plumps over a few hours and the color reads like 'your lips if you'd just been lightly making out.' We tested it against six competitors and it won on wear, hydration, and not looking like you're trying. *We only feature things we'd actually use on our own face.*

last_word.quote: The mouth is the front door of the face.
last_word.attribution: — Dr. Linda Lee, Johns Hopkins gastroenterologist, in a March 30 NYT interview about the oral-gut axis
